purpose: Single source of truth for Project Dokkaebi enterprise-readiness evaluation, issue generation, worktree execution, pull request review, merge, and re-evaluation.

designStandards.name: Carbon Design System color guidance
requiredInterpretation: Use docs/design/carbon-ui-baseline.md as the repository-owned interpretation for UI PRs.
Use role-based color tokens rather than hard-coded product colors for UI surfaces.
Use Carbon light and dark theme layering rules when Dokkaebi introduces product UI.
Use the core blue action family intentionally, with support colors reserved for status and alerts.
Require accessible contrast for text, icons, graphical elements, focus states, and data visualization.

scoringScale.0: Required for company use but absent or not evidenced.
scoringScale.1-30: Direction exists, but the implementation or operating evidence is too thin for company use.
scoringScale.31-60: Partially implemented, mostly manual, sandbox-oriented, or not yet auditable enough.
scoringScale.61-80: Usable for internal pilots, with important enterprise controls still missing.
scoringScale.81-95: Strong enough to become an internal standard after targeted proof and automation.
scoringScale.96-100: Production-ready, auditable, supportable, and validated against realistic operating scenarios.

workflow.sourceOfTruth: This file is the readiness criteria source of truth. GitHub Project Status remains the lifecycle state source of truth for work items.
workflow.phaseModel: For each area below, open or update a GitHub issue from nextIssues as a backlog candidate. The issue becomes dispatchable only after it is added to an approved GitHub Project with lifecycle/admission fields. Then create a short-lived worktree and branch, implement with evidence, open a pull request, merge only after required validation, and update currentPercent and nextIssues from the new evidence.
workflow.issueContract: Each issue must cite the relevant area id, scope, currentPercent, targetPercent, acceptance criteria, validation requirements, authority requirements, metadata hygiene expectation, and result evidence expected from the worker.
workflow.worktreeContract: Each issue is solved in an isolated worktree or worker workspace. Branch names follow docs/policies/git-governance.md and do not use actor prefixes.
workflow.pullRequestContract: Each pull request must include context, decision, why, validation, residual risks, git status, and issue linkage.
workflow.mergeContract: Merge only after the applicable repository checks pass and approval requirements in docs/policies/git-governance.md are satisfied.
workflow.evaluationLoop: After merge, re-run the relevant validation, reassess all criteria from evidence, create follow-up issues for any area or critical capability below 100%, and repeat until every area and critical capability reaches 100%.
workflow.selfImprovementContract: The readiness loop may change its own criteria, validation, issue contract, or phase model when evidence shows the loop is failing to drive work, omits required enterprise evidence, creates ambiguous issues, cannot safely score a criterion, or repeatedly blocks on the same operational gap.
improvementTriggers: A readiness issue is not dispatchable because required scope, authority, validation, result evidence, or metadata hygiene is missing.
A criterion cannot be scored from repository, GitHub, CI, runtime, or operations evidence.
A validator passes while a documented loop requirement is absent or unenforced.
A review, incident, or failed PR identifies a repeatable gap in the loop itself.
The loop reaches an enterprise standard that is undefined and requires a Human decision before scoring can continue.
improvementEvidence: Open or update a GitHub issue that names the loop gap and its acceptance criteria.
Capture RED evidence showing the missing or weak loop behavior before changing criteria or validation.
Make the smallest criteria, documentation, issue-template, or validator change that closes the gap.
Run readiness, contract, package, git-governance, and relevant targeted validation before PR.
Open and merge a PR before using the improved loop to rescore readiness.

metadataHygiene.publicBranchRule: Use <type>/<scope-slug> branch names without actor prefixes.
metadataHygiene.publicCommitRule: Commit messages must explain process, decision, validation, and residual risk without exposing internal automation labels.
metadataHygiene.publicIssueRule: Issue and pull request metadata must describe product work, not private tool execution details.

areas.name: Architecture and contracts
areas.enterpriseStandard: Manager, Fire, Hammer, GitHub Project state, credential, approval, and result-packet boundaries are complete, versioned, validated, and exercised by at least one live or replayed operating scenario.
evaluationQuestions: Can a new Manager implementation pass the contract without reading private project memory?
Can a worker result packet be rejected deterministically when required evidence is missing?
Do contract validators catch drift across architecture, workflow, policy, and templates?
evidenceRequired: Example accepted and rejected worker result packets

areas.name: Core orchestration
areas.enterpriseStandard: Fire reliably discovers, admits, dispatches, monitors, retries, and closes work without duplicate execution, stale state, or silent loss across restarts.
evaluationQuestions: Can Fire restart without duplicate dispatch or lost retry intent?
Can worker route selection explain why each target was selected or skipped?
Can failed workers produce actionable state and evidence for Human Review?
evidenceRequired: Unit and integration tests for admission, route selection, retry, and closeout

areas.name: Infrastructure and platform
areas.enterpriseStandard: Development, sandbox, staging, and production topologies are reproducible, isolated, monitored, backed up, and recoverable with least-privilege worker routes.
evaluationQuestions: Can a new environment be rebuilt from documented commands and checked state?
Are local, SSH, Docker, and Kubernetes workers registered with clear capabilities and cleanup rules?
Can Fire recover its state and workspaces after host or process failure?

areas.name: Development and quality
areas.enterpriseStandard: Every change has focused tests, deterministic validation, risk-based coverage, release evidence, and regression protection for adjacent contracts.
evaluationQuestions: Does every readiness criterion map to an executable or reviewable validation?
Are high-risk runtime paths covered by tests that fail for the right reason?
Can a release candidate be verified and rolled back from documented evidence?
evidenceRequired: Targeted test output for changed areas

areas.name: Security and authority
areas.enterpriseStandard: All credentials, approvals, worker capabilities, high-risk operations, audit events, and emergency paths are least-privilege, revocable, observable, and fail-closed.
evaluationQuestions: Can each credential grant be traced to an approval boundary and revoked?
Can a worker perform only the operations authorized by its ticket and route?
Are high-impact operations blocked without explicit Human approval evidence?

areas.name: Management and governance
areas.enterpriseStandard: GitHub Project fields, issue intake, Human approval, PR review, result packets, and closeout reconciliation are governed by reproducible policy and auditable roles.
evaluationQuestions: Can a non-automation team member understand why an item is in its current state?
Can role boundaries explain who may authorize, dispatch, merge, and audit work?
Can mismatches between issue status, PR state, and result evidence be detected?

areas.name: Logging and observability
areas.enterpriseStandard: State, logs, metrics, traces, alerts, dashboards, and audit exports let operators detect, diagnose, prove, and improve system behavior without reading private worker memory.
evaluationQuestions: Can an operator identify stalled dispatch, unhealthy workers, and credential denial within minutes?
Can metrics and logs be correlated by issue, route, worker, session, and commit?
Can audit records be exported without leaking secrets?
evidenceRequired: SEV1 and SEV2 delivery receipt evidence
Quiet-hours routing and escalation receipt evidence

areas.name: Operations and SRE
areas.enterpriseStandard: The service has SLOs, incident response, on-call paths, runbooks, rollout and rollback procedures, capacity planning, and routine drills.
evaluationQuestions: What happens when Fire is down, GitHub API is rate-limited, or all workers are unhealthy?
Who is paged and what runbook do they follow?
What are acceptable dispatch latency and recovery objectives?

areas.name: Compliance and audit
areas.enterpriseStandard: The system can produce control-mapped, retained, tamper-evident evidence for approvals, credentials, code changes, worker execution, and operational incidents.
evaluationQuestions: Can an auditor reconstruct who approved a worker action and why?
Can evidence be exported with redaction and integrity proof?
Do policies map to common enterprise controls?
evidenceRequired: Approved immutable audit storage sandbox gate

areas.name: Productization and UX
areas.enterpriseStandard: Admins, approvers, operators, auditors, and worker authors can install, configure, monitor, approve, troubleshoot, and learn the product without relying on undocumented operator context.
evaluationQuestions: Can a new admin install and configure a sandbox without maintainer help?
Can a Human reviewer approve or reject work from GitHub issue and PR surfaces confidently?
Can an operator troubleshoot worker failures from documented symptoms?
evidenceRequired: Guided onboarding UI screenshots and browser action log
Admin UI or CLI screenshots when available
CI-regenerated onboarding visual matrix across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit
Reviewable GitHub Actions summary and uploaded browser action logs

areas.name: Design system and visual quality
areas.enterpriseStandard: All Dokkaebi UI surfaces use a documented Carbon Design System baseline for color, interaction states, accessibility, and enterprise information density.
evaluationQuestions: Do UI surfaces use Carbon tokens and theme layering instead of arbitrary palettes?
Do focus, hover, selected, active, disabled, error, warning, and success states meet accessibility requirements?
Does the interface feel like a dense operational product rather than a marketing page?
evidenceRequired: Desktop and mobile visual QA captures

areas.name: Kubernetes Fire and Hammer platformization

subCriteria.name: K8S loop contract and issue publication
Kustomize, namespace, RBAC, ServiceAccount, and NetworkPolicy baseline
Admission deny fixture matrix and exact expected failures
Accepted route-profile fixtures for every approved Hammer ServiceAccount
Disposable Kubernetes API server admission and RBAC proof
Fire Deployment startup and approved Hammer Job creation smoke
gaps: Fire Deployment packaging and startup smoke are not yet proven against a running Kubernetes target.
Fire has not yet created only approved Hammer Jobs from a Kubernetes runtime surface.
subCriteria.nextIssueTitle: Package Fire Deployment smoke with least-privilege Job orchestration

subCriteria.name: Hammer Job profile runtime smoke and route-result metadata
gaps: Hammer profiles have static accepted fixtures but no executed Job smoke that captures route profile, namespace, ServiceAccount, image digest, exit status, log surface, and cleanup result.
Breakglass remains intentionally inactive pending later ADR and Human approval.
subCriteria.nextIssueTitle: Prove Hammer Job profile smoke and route-result metadata

subCriteria.name: Kubernetes Job, GitHub lifecycle, log, PR/check, and result-packet reconciliation
gaps: Reconciliation is represented as a local replay, not yet proven against a running Fire/Hammer route.
Stale or failed Job closeout handling is not yet exercised with Kubernetes status and logs.
subCriteria.nextIssueTitle: Define K8S result packet and GitHub lifecycle reconciliation

subCriteria.name: EKS workload identity and Secret boundary
gaps: EKS workload identity strategy is intentionally deferred until live AWS/EKS evidence is approved.
Secret exceptions, if any, still require Human approval, reason, expiry, and cleanup evidence.
subCriteria.nextIssueTitle: Decide EKS workload identity and Secret boundary

areas.enterpriseStandard: Dokkaebi Fire runs inside Kubernetes with least-privilege Job orchestration, Hammer runs as one scoped Job per ticket, admission/RBAC/NetworkPolicy enforce route boundaries, and GitHub Project lifecycle, result packets, PRs, logs, and audit evidence reconcile without trusting issue prose.
gaps: Fire Deployment packaging and startup smoke remain future work.
Hammer Job profiles have static route fixtures but are not yet executed as Jobs with route-result metadata and cleanup evidence.
GitHub Project, Kubernetes Job status, logs, and result-packet reconciliation is locally replayed but not yet proven against a running Fire/Hammer route.
EKS workload identity and Secret boundary decision is documented as deferred, but the final EKS identity strategy is not yet selected from live evidence.
evaluationQuestions: Can Fire create only approved Hammer Jobs while being unable to read Secrets or mutate RBAC, namespaces, CRDs, nodes, persistent volumes, or production application resources?
Can each Hammer route profile prove least privilege, result evidence, and cleanup without sharing a broad ServiceAccount?
Can admission reject malformed or over-privileged Job requests even when issue text or result packets are prompt-injection surfaces?
Can the Manager reconcile GitHub Project lifecycle state, Kubernetes Job state, logs, PR/check state, and result packet evidence before closeout?
evidenceRequired: Kustomize base and overlays for namespaces, ServiceAccounts, RBAC, and NetworkPolicy
Static RBAC and loop validator output
Accepted route-profile fixtures for every approved Hammer ServiceAccount
Disposable Kubernetes API server admission and RBAC proof
Result packet and Job status reconciliation replay
EKS identity and Secret access boundary record
Fire-in-K8S startup and approved Hammer Job creation smoke
Hammer Job profile can/cannot matrix against a live or approved sandbox route

nextIssues.title: Define K8S admission policy gate for Hammer Jobs
acceptanceCriteria: Accepted Job fixture includes ticket id, tenant id, approval id, route profile, credential grant id, approved image/profile, resource requests and limits, result packet sink, namespace, and ServiceAccount match.

Rejected fixtures cover missing or empty approval, ticket, and credential labels, missing result packet sink, invalid, duplicate-invalid, duplicate-empty, bare-prefix, initContainer, and ephemeralContainer result packet sinks, mismatched ServiceAccount/profile, repo-local wrong apiVersion or kind fixture schema, unapproved image/profile, empty resources, hostPath, privileged containers, capabilities.add, hostNetwork, hostPort, hostPID, hostIPC, shareProcessNamespace, broad volume mount, missing/default-root pod and container security contexts, pod-level root execution, initContainer privilege, ephemeralContainer privilege, ephemeral Secret env/root mount bypass, imagePullSecrets, projected service account token, projected Secret, CSI secret-store volume, hammer-no-k8s token override, overlay traversal, unauthorized RBAC expansion, and Secret access.
Validation fails closed without live cluster mutation.
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: Docs-only by default. Live admission controller, Kubernetes API server, EKS, cloud, credential, deployment, production, worker, Docker, remote host, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.
resultEvidence: RED denied-fixture evidence and GREEN validator output

nextIssues.title: Package Fire Deployment smoke with least-privilege Job orchestration
acceptanceCriteria: Fire manifest references only approved non-secret configuration and a brokered credential path.
Fire ServiceAccount can create/get/list/watch/delete Jobs and read Job Pods, Pod logs, Events, and non-secret ConfigMaps only in approved namespaces.
Fire cannot get/list/watch Secrets and cannot mutate RBAC, namespaces, CRDs, nodes, persistent volumes, or production application resources.
Smoke plan names the exact later command that proves Fire starts, reads GitHub Project configuration, creates only an approved Hammer Job, and writes result evidence back to the configured surface.
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: Docs-only by default. Live Kubernetes, EKS, cloud, credential, deployment, production, worker, Docker, remote host, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

nextIssues.title: Prove Hammer Job profile smoke and route-result metadata
acceptanceCriteria: hammer-no-k8s has no Kubernetes API token mounted and cannot override the no-token boundary through the Job pod spec.
Every Kubernetes-capable Hammer profile has a named namespace, ServiceAccount, resource limits, image/profile, result packet sink, cleanup rule, and approval boundary.
Breakglass remains unbound and inactive unless a later ADR and Human approval define expiry, reason, audit, and cleanup.
Result packet evidence names route profile, namespace, ServiceAccount, image digest, exit status, log surface, validation output, and cleanup result.
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: Docs-only by default. Live worker, Kubernetes, Docker, credential, deployment, production, remote host, cloud, EKS, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.
resultEvidence: Result packet schema update if needed

nextIssues.title: Define K8S result packet and GitHub lifecycle reconciliation
acceptanceCriteria: Reconciliation rejects Done when GitHub Project status, Job status, logs, result packet, PR/check state, or approval evidence disagree.
Stale or failed Jobs become follow-up issues or Fix Requested with preserved route/result metadata.
Closeout evidence remains durable outside Manager memory.
A local replay proves the reject/accept matrix without live external writes.
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: Docs-only by default. Live GitHub Project, Kubernetes, credential, worker, Docker, remote host, deployment, production, cloud, or EKS mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

nextIssues.title: Decide EKS workload identity and Secret boundary
acceptanceCriteria: Decision record names why the selected identity strategy fits Fire, Hammer, credential broker, tenant RBAC, and audit requirements.
Secret access remains default denied; any named Secret exception requires Human approval, reason, expiry, and cleanup evidence.

Hammer prompts, issue bodies, result packets, Job env, logs, and artifacts must not include raw Manager PATs, OAuth tokens, SSH private keys, kubeconfigs, cloud credentials, or GitHub App private keys.
Validation fails closed on broad Secret read/list/watch or missing approval boundary text.
nextIssues.authorityRequirement: Docs-only by default. Live AWS, EKS, IAM, credential, Kubernetes, production, deployment, worker, Docker, remote host, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

criticalCapabilities.name: Incident Response
criticalCapabilities.nextIssueTitle: Completed incident response drill and postmortem exercise
acceptanceCriteria: Run an approved incident response drill or sandbox exercise using the dedicated runbook, SRE baseline, and on-call paging baseline.

Capture detection input, severity declaration, commander assignment, communication timeline, mitigation decision, rollback or recovery decision, alert routing decision, validation output, cleanup, and postmortem evidence.
Prove the exercise fails closed when commander, communication, mitigation, approval-gate status, or postmortem evidence is missing.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only from captured approved drill or sandbox evidence.
criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: Completed through approved docs-only local drill evidence. Sandbox, worker, credential, infrastructure, Docker, Kubernetes, remote host, deployment, production, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation remains explicitly not authorized without Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

criticalCapabilities.name: On-call, paging, and alerting
criticalCapabilities.nextIssueTitle: Completed approved on-call delivery sandbox gate
acceptanceCriteria: Approved local sandbox substitute routes representative SEV1 and SEV2 alerts.
Alert input, routing decision, quiet-hours decision, delivery receipt, escalation receipt, cleanup receipt, residual risk, and next action are captured.

Live alerting service, live paging service, metrics service, credentials, infrastructure, workers, remote hosts, Docker, Kubernetes, deployment, production, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation remain separately approval-gated.
Readiness is updated only from captured approved sandbox delivery evidence.
criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: Completed through approved local sandbox on-call delivery evidence only. Live alerting service, live paging service, metrics service, credentials, infrastructure, workers, remote hosts, Docker, Kubernetes, deployment, production, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation remain separately approval-gated under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

acceptanceCriteria: Capture approved sandbox or explicitly Human-approved live-backend dispatch-latency, recovery-time, review-age, and availability posture evidence using docs/operations/service-level-objectives.md and docs/operations/central-metrics-backend.md.

Capture metric query output, fallback evidence, error-budget result, alert linkage, approval-gate status, cleanup, residual risk, and next action from approved sandbox or explicitly Human-approved live backend evidence.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only after approved sandbox or explicitly Human-approved live backend SLO evidence exists.

criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: Completed through approved local sandbox metrics backend evidence. criticalCapabilities.name: Backup, restore, and disaster recovery
acceptanceCriteria: Add an executable durable backup verification path for project exports, lease/retry state, route-result summaries, and evidence packages.
Capture restore verification output, measured RPO and RTO, retention policy checks, redaction checks, approval-gate status, cleanup receipt, residual risk, and next action.
Update docs/enterprise-readiness/criteria.json only from captured local, approved sandbox, or explicitly Human-approved runtime evidence.
criticalCapabilities.authorityRequirement: Docs-and-local-validation by default. Approved-sandbox-validation requires exact target approval. Runtime, remote host, Docker, Kubernetes, Proxmox, credential, production, deployment, infrastructure, or GitHub Project control-plane mutation requires explicit Human approval under docs/policies/authority-and-safety.md.

criticalCapabilities.name: Compliance package
criticalCapabilities.nextIssueTitle: Completed approved immutable audit storage sandbox gate

Stored signed manifest is verified from retained public-key metadata and recorded manifest hash evidence.
Retention enforcement, legal-hold state, deletion or extension decision, owner review, redaction review, cleanup, residual risk, and next action are captured.

Live immutable storage, production retention service, production signing service, credentials, infrastructure, workers, remote hosts, Docker, Kubernetes, deployment, production, and GitHub Project control-plane mutation remain separately approval-gated.
